{{Japanese clan |Name = Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō |Crest = |Crest Name = |Main Surname = {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} |Founder = {{ill|Inagimi no Mikoto|ja|印岐美命}} |Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Tenson|ja|天孫}}) |Origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} |Base = Tōtsuōmi Province (later {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}) |Notable Figures = See {{section link|#Notable Figures}} |Branches = (Parentheses denote clan suffix)
{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}
{{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|久努国造}}
{{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久野氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kuji Kokuzō|ja|久自国造}}
{{ill|Ōbe clan|ja|大部氏}} (Zō) }} '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō''' (遠淡海国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Tōtsuōmi Province, which encompassed the western region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. The title is also rendered as Tōtōmi Kokuzō. == Overview == * The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} mentions the title as '''Tōtōmi Kokuzō'''. The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} refers to it as '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō'''. The progenitor of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō is recorded as {{ill|Takeruhiratori no Mikoto|ja|建比良鳥命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Amatsuhikohikoho no Ninigi|ja|天之菩卑能命}}, linking the lineage to the {{ill|Izumo Kokuzō|ja|出雲国造}}, {{ill|Musashi Kokuzō|ja|武蔵国造}}, and other related clans. * The ''Kokuzō Hongi'' of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' names {{ill|Inagimi no Mikoto|ja|印岐美命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ikashikone no Mikoto|ja|伊香色雄命}}, as the Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' states that Inagimi no Mikoto, the brother of {{ill|Katakatane no Mikoto|ja|片堅石命}}, founder of {{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}, was appointed Kokuzō of Tōtsuōmi. This record also describes Inagimi as the ancestor of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō, and several related clans.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan belonged to the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}. While the clan's official {{ill|uji|ja|氏}} and {{ill|kabane|ja|姓}} remain uncertain, it is believed to have adopted '''Tōtsuōmi''' (遠淡海氏) as its name, with the kabane of '''Nao''' (直), like its related clans {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久努氏}} and {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}."[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/mononobe2g.htm Overview of the Mononobe Clan]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2003. The clan is also associated with the use of "Tachibana" (橘) and the mythical rejuvenating "Ochimizu" (変若水). Several descendants later adopted the {{ill|Tachibana no Ason|ja|橘朝臣}} surname.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/monobekz/monobek1.htm Examination of the Mononobe Lineage in the ''Tenson Hongi'']," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2008. == Headquarters == === Main Base === The headquarters of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō were in {{ill|Iwata District, Tōtōmi|ja|磐田郡}}, corresponding to modern {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市}}. This area later became the site of the provincial {{ill|kokufu|ja|国府}} and {{ill|kokubunji|ja|国分寺}} of Tōtōmi Province. === Territory === The Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō governed the region known as Tōtsuōmi, later divided into Iwata District and surrounding areas, including {{ill|Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|ja|山香郡}}, {{ill|Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|ja|長上郡}}, and {{ill|Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|ja|長下郡}}. The boundaries of the Kokuzō's territory remain debated, with theories including: * Surroundings of {{ill|Lake Hamana|ja|浜名湖}}. * West of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. * From Lake Hamana to the western edge of {{ill|Iwata Plain|ja|磐田原}}. * From Lake Hamana to the {{ill|Ōtagawa River|ja|太田川}} basin. == Clan Deity == === Shrine Associations === * '''{{ill|Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|ja|淡海國玉神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mitsuke, Iwata|ja|磐田市見付}}, this shrine is the {{ill|sōja|ja|惣社}} of Tōtōmi Province and enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}. * '''{{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小国神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|ja|森町}}, this shrine is the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of Tōtōmi Province, enshrining {{ill|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴神}}. === Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Kochōshizuka Kofun|ja|小銚子塚古墳}}''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten,'' {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}. * Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan. * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社}}. * ''Daijiten of Shintō,'' {{ill|Rinsen Bookstore|ja|臨川書店}}. * ''General Overview of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] {{ill|Ōbe clan|ja|大部氏}} (Zō) }} '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō''' (遠淡海国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Tōtsuōmi Province, which encompassed the western region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. The title is also rendered as Tōtōmi Kokuzō. == Overview == * The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} mentions the title as '''Tōtōmi Kokuzō'''. The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} refers to it as '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō'''. The progenitor of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō is recorded as {{ill|Takeruhiratori no Mikoto|ja|建比良鳥命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Amatsuhikohikoho no Ninigi|ja|天之菩卑能命}}, linking the lineage to the {{ill|Izumo Kokuzō|ja|出雲国造}}, {{ill|Musashi Kokuzō|ja|武蔵国造}}, and other related clans. * The ''Kokuzō Hongi'' of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' names {{ill|Inagimi no Mikoto|ja|印岐美命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ikashikone no Mikoto|ja|伊香色雄命}}, as the Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' states that Inagimi no Mikoto, the brother of {{ill|Katakatane no Mikoto|ja|片堅石命}}, founder of {{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}, was appointed Kokuzō of Tōtsuōmi. This record also describes Inagimi as the ancestor of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō, and several related clans.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan belonged to the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}. While the clan's official {{ill|uji|ja|氏}} and {{ill|kabane|ja|姓}} remain uncertain, it is believed to have adopted '''Tōtsuōmi''' (遠淡海氏) as its name, with the kabane of '''Nao''' (直), like its related clans {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久努氏}} and {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}."[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/mononobe2g.htm Overview of the Mononobe Clan]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2003. The clan is also associated with the use of "Tachibana" (橘) and the mythical rejuvenating "Ochimizu" (変若水). Several descendants later adopted the {{ill|Tachibana no Ason|ja|橘朝臣}} surname.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/monobekz/monobek1.htm Examination of the Mononobe Lineage in the ''Tenson Hongi'']," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2008. == Headquarters == === Main Base === The headquarters of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō were in {{ill|Iwata District, Tōtōmi|ja|磐田郡}}, corresponding to modern {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市}}. This area later became the site of the provincial {{ill|kokufu|ja|国府}} and {{ill|kokubunji|ja|国分寺}} of Tōtōmi Province. === Territory === The Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō governed the region known as Tōtsuōmi, later divided into Iwata District and surrounding areas, including {{ill|Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|ja|山香郡}}, {{ill|Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|ja|長上郡}}, and {{ill|Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|ja|長下郡}}. The boundaries of the Kokuzō's territory remain debated, with theories including: * Surroundings of {{ill|Lake Hamana|ja|浜名湖}}. * West of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. * From Lake Hamana to the western edge of {{ill|Iwata Plain|ja|磐田原}}. * From Lake Hamana to the {{ill|Ōtagawa River|ja|太田川}} basin. == Clan Deity == === Shrine Associations === * '''{{ill|Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|ja|淡海國玉神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mitsuke, Iwata|ja|磐田市見付}}, this shrine is the {{ill|sōja|ja|惣社}} of Tōtōmi Province and enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}. * '''{{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小国神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|ja|森町}}, this shrine is the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of Tōtōmi Province, enshrining {{ill|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴神}}. === Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Kochōshizuka Kofun|ja|小銚子塚古墳}}''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten,'' {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}. * Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan. * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社}}. * ''Daijiten of Shintō,'' {{ill|Rinsen Bookstore|ja|臨川書店}}. * ''General Overview of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] {{Japanese clan |Name = Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō |Crest = |Crest Name = |Main Surname = {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} |Founder = {{ill|Inagimi no Mikoto|ja|印岐美命}} |Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Tenson|ja|天孫}}) |Origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} |Base = Tōtsuōmi Province (later {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}) |Notable Figures = See {{section link|#Notable Figures}} |Branches = (Parentheses denote clan suffix)
{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}
{{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|久努国造}}
{{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久野氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kuji Kokuzō|ja|久自国造}}
{{ill|Ōbe clan|ja|大部氏}} (Zō) }} '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō''' (遠淡海国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Tōtsuōmi Province, which encompassed the western region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. The title is also rendered as Tōtōmi Kokuzō. == Overview == * The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} mentions the title as '''Tōtōmi Kokuzō'''. The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} refers to it as '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō'''. The progenitor of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō is recorded as {{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|Takehi-Nateru=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Takeruhiratori no Mikoto}}, a descendant of {{ill|Amatsuhikohikoho no Ninigi|ja|天之菩卑能命}}, linking the lineage to the {{ill|Izumo Kokuzō|ja|出雲国造}}, {{ill|Musashi Kokuzō|ja|武蔵国造}}, and other related clans. * The ''Kokuzō Hongi'' of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' names {{ill|Inagimi no Mikoto|ja|印岐美命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ikashikone no Mikoto|ja|伊香色雄命}}, as the Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' states that Inagimi no Mikoto, the brother of {{ill|Katakatane no Mikoto|ja|片堅石命}}, founder of {{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}, was appointed Kokuzō of Tōtsuōmi. This record also describes Inagimi as the ancestor of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō, and several related clans.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan belonged to the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}. While the clan's official {{ill|uji|ja|氏}} and {{ill|kabane|ja|姓}} remain uncertain, it is believed to have adopted '''Tōtsuōmi''' (遠淡海氏) as its name, with the kabane of '''Nao''' (直), like its related clans {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久努氏}} and {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}."[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/mononobe2g.htm Overview of the Mononobe Clan]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2003. The clan is also associated with the use of "Tachibana" (橘) and the mythical rejuvenating "Ochimizu" (変若水). Several descendants later adopted the {{ill|Tachibana no Ason|ja|橘朝臣}} surname.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/monobekz/monobek1.htm Examination of the Mononobe Lineage in the ''Tenson Hongi'']," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2008. == Headquarters == === Main Base === The headquarters of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō were in {{ill|Iwata District, Tōtōmi|ja|磐田郡}}, corresponding to modern {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市}}. This area later became the site of the provincial {{ill|kokufu|ja|国府}} and {{ill|kokubunji|ja|国分寺}} of Tōtōmi Province. === Territory === The Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō governed the region known as Tōtsuōmi, later divided into Iwata District and surrounding areas, including {{ill|Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|ja|山香郡}}, {{ill|Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|ja|長上郡}}, and {{ill|Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|ja|長下郡}}. The boundaries of the Kokuzō's territory remain debated, with theories including: * Surroundings of {{ill|Lake Hamana|ja|浜名湖}}. * West of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. * From Lake Hamana to the western edge of {{ill|Iwata Plain|ja|磐田原}}. * From Lake Hamana to the {{ill|Ōtagawa River|ja|太田川}} basin. == Clan Deity == === Shrine Associations === * '''{{ill|Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|ja|淡海國玉神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mitsuke, Iwata|ja|磐田市見付}}, this shrine is the {{ill|sōja|ja|惣社}} of Tōtōmi Province and enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}. * '''{{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小国神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|ja|森町}}, this shrine is the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of Tōtōmi Province, enshrining {{ill|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴神}}. === Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Kochōshizuka Kofun|ja|小銚子塚古墳}}''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten,'' {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}. * Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan. * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社}}. * ''Daijiten of Shintō,'' {{ill|Rinsen Bookstore|ja|臨川書店}}. * ''General Overview of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] The title is also rendered as Tōtōmi Kokuzō. == Overview == * The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} mentions the title as '''Tōtōmi Kokuzō'''. The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} refers to it as '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō'''. The progenitor of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō is recorded as {{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|Takehi-Nateru=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Takeruhiratori no Mikoto}}, a descendant of [[Ame no Hohi]], linking the lineage to the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|出雲国造}}, {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造|lt=Musashi Kokuzō}}, and other related clans. * The ''Kokuzō Hongi'' of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' names {{ill|Inagimi no Mikoto|ja|印岐美命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}}, as the Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' states that Inagimi no Mikoto, the brother of {{ill|Katakatane no Mikoto|ja|片堅石命}}, founder of {{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}, was appointed Kokuzō of Tōtsuōmi. This record also describes Inagimi as the ancestor of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō, and several related clans.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan belonged to the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}. While the clan's official {{ill|uji|ja|氏}} and {{ill|kabane|ja|姓}} remain uncertain, it is believed to have adopted '''Tōtsuōmi''' (遠淡海氏) as its name, with the kabane of '''Nao''' (直), like its related clans {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久努氏}} and {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}."[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/mononobe2g.htm Overview of the Mononobe Clan]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2003. The clan is also associated with the use of "Tachibana" (橘) and the mythical rejuvenating "Ochimizu" (変若水). Several descendants later adopted the {{ill|Tachibana no Ason|ja|橘朝臣}} surname.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/monobekz/monobek1.htm Examination of the Mononobe Lineage in the ''Tenson Hongi'']," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2008. == Headquarters == === Main Base === The headquarters of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō were in {{ill|Iwata District, Tōtōmi|ja|磐田郡}}, corresponding to modern {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市}}. This area later became the site of the provincial {{ill|kokufu|ja|国府}} and {{ill|kokubunji|ja|国分寺}} of Tōtōmi Province. === Territory === The Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō governed the region known as Tōtsuōmi, later divided into Iwata District and surrounding areas, including {{ill|Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|ja|山香郡}}, {{ill|Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|ja|長上郡}}, and {{ill|Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|ja|長下郡}}. The boundaries of the Kokuzō's territory remain debated, with theories including: * Surroundings of {{ill|Lake Hamana|ja|浜名湖}}. * West of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. * From Lake Hamana to the western edge of {{ill|Iwata Plain|ja|磐田原}}. * From Lake Hamana to the {{ill|Ōtagawa River|ja|太田川}} basin. == Clan Deity == === Shrine Associations === * '''{{ill|Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|ja|淡海國玉神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mitsuke, Iwata|ja|磐田市見付}}, this shrine is the {{ill|sōja|ja|惣社}} of Tōtōmi Province and enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}. * '''{{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小国神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|ja|森町}}, this shrine is the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of Tōtōmi Province, enshrining {{ill|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴神}}. === Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Kochōshizuka Kofun|ja|小銚子塚古墳}}''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten,'' {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}. * Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan. * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社}}. * ''Daijiten of Shintō,'' {{ill|Rinsen Bookstore|ja|臨川書店}}. * ''General Overview of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] {{Japanese clan |Name = Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō |Crest = |Crest Name = |Main Surname = {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} |Founder = {{ill|Inagimi no Mikoto|ja|印岐美命}} |Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Tenson|ja|天孫}}) |Origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} |Base = Tōtsuōmi Province (later {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}) |Notable Figures = See {{section link|#Notable Figures}} |Branches = (Parentheses denote clan suffix)
{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}
{{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|久努国造}}
{{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久野氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kuji Kokuzō|ja|久自国造}}
{{ill|Ōbe clan|ja|大部氏}} (Zō) }} '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō''' (遠淡海国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Tōtsuōmi Province, which encompassed the western region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. The title is also rendered as Tōtōmi Kokuzō. == Overview == * The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} mentions the title as '''Tōtōmi Kokuzō'''. The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} refers to it as '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō'''. The progenitor of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō is recorded as {{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|Takehi-Nateru=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Takeruhiratori no Mikoto}}, a descendant of [[Ame no Hohi]], linking the lineage to the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|出雲国造}}, {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造|lt=Musashi Kokuzō}}, and other related clans. * The ''Kokuzō Hongi'' of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' names {{ill|Inagimi no Mikoto|ja|印岐美命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}}, as the Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' states that Inagimi no Mikoto, the brother of {{ill|Katakatane no Mikoto|ja|片堅石命}}, founder of {{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}, was appointed Kokuzō of Tōtsuōmi. This record also describes Inagimi as the ancestor of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō, and several related clans.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan belonged to the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}. While the clan's official {{ill|uji|ja|氏}} and {{ill|kabane|ja|姓}} remain uncertain, it is believed to have adopted '''Tōtsuōmi''' (遠淡海氏) as its name, with the kabane of '''Nao''' (直), like its related clans {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久努氏}} and {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}."[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/mononobe2g.htm Overview of the Mononobe Clan]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2003. The clan is also associated with the use of "Tachibana" (橘) and the mythical rejuvenating "Ochimizu" (変若水). Several descendants later adopted the {{ill|Tachibana no Ason|ja|橘朝臣}} surname.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/monobekz/monobek1.htm Examination of the Mononobe Lineage in the ''Tenson Hongi'']," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2008. == Headquarters == === Main Base === The headquarters of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō were in {{ill|Iwata District, Tōtōmi|ja|磐田郡}}, corresponding to modern {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市}}. This area later became the site of the provincial {{ill|kokufu|ja|国府}} and {{ill|Provincial temple|ja|国分寺}} of Tōtōmi Province. === Territory === The Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō governed the region known as Tōtsuōmi, later divided into Iwata District and surrounding areas, including {{ill|Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|ja|山香郡}}, {{ill|Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|ja|長上郡}}, and {{ill|Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|ja|長下郡}}. The boundaries of the Kokuzō's territory remain debated, with theories including: * Surroundings of {{ill|Lake Hamana|ja|浜名湖}}. * West of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. * From Lake Hamana to the western edge of {{ill|Iwata Plain|ja|磐田原}}. * From Lake Hamana to the {{ill|Ōtagawa River|ja|太田川}} basin. == Clan Deity == === Shrine Associations === * '''{{ill|Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|ja|淡海國玉神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mitsuke, Iwata|ja|磐田市見付}}, this shrine is the {{ill|sōja|ja|惣社}} of Tōtōmi Province and enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}. * '''{{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小国神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|ja|森町}}, this shrine is the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of Tōtōmi Province, enshrining {{ill|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴神}}. === Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Kochōshizuka Kofun|ja|小銚子塚古墳}}''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten,'' {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}. * Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan. * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社}}. * ''Daijiten of Shintō,'' {{ill|Rinsen Bookstore|ja|臨川書店}}. * ''General Overview of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] {{ill|Ōbe clan|ja|大部氏}} (Zō) }} '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō''' (遠淡海国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Tōtsuōmi Province, which encompassed the western region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. The title is also rendered as Tōtōmi Kokuzō. == Overview == * The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} mentions the title as '''Tōtōmi Kokuzō'''. The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} refers to it as '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō'''. The progenitor of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō is recorded as {{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|Takehi-Nateru=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Takeruhiratori no Mikoto}}, a descendant of [[Ame no Hohi]], linking the lineage to the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|出雲国造}}, {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造|lt=Musashi Kokuzō}}, and other related clans. * The ''Kokuzō Hongi'' of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' names {{ill|Inagimi no Mikoto|ja|印岐美命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}}, as the Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' states that Inagimi no Mikoto, the brother of {{ill|Katakatane no Mikoto|ja|片堅石命}}, founder of {{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}, was appointed Kokuzō of Tōtsuōmi. This record also describes Inagimi as the ancestor of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō, and several related clans.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan belonged to the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}. While the clan's official {{ill|uji|ja|氏}} and {{ill|kabane|ja|姓}} remain uncertain, it is believed to have adopted '''Tōtsuōmi''' (遠淡海氏) as its name, with the kabane of '''Nao''' (直), like its related clans {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久努氏}} and {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}."[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/mononobe2g.htm Overview of the Mononobe Clan]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2003. The clan is also associated with the use of "Tachibana" (橘) and the mythical rejuvenating "Ochimizu" (変若水). Several descendants later adopted the {{ill|Tachibana no Ason|ja|橘朝臣}} surname.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/monobekz/monobek1.htm Examination of the Mononobe Lineage in the ''Tenson Hongi'']," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2008. == Headquarters == === Main Base === The headquarters of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō were in {{ill|Iwata District, Tōtōmi|ja|磐田郡}}, corresponding to modern {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市}}. This area later became the site of the provincial {{ill|kokufu|ja|国府}} and {{ill|Provincial temple|ja|国分寺}} of Tōtōmi Province. === Territory === The Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō governed the region known as Tōtsuōmi, later divided into Iwata District and surrounding areas, including {{ill|Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|ja|山香郡}}, {{ill|Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|ja|長上郡}}, and {{ill|Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|ja|長下郡}}. The boundaries of the Kokuzō's territory remain debated, with theories including: * Surroundings of {{ill|Lake Hamana|ja|浜名湖}}. * West of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. * From Lake Hamana to the western edge of {{ill|Iwata Plain|ja|磐田原}}. * From Lake Hamana to the {{ill|Ōtagawa River|ja|太田川}} basin. == Clan Deity == === Shrine Associations === * '''{{ill|Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|ja|淡海國玉神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mitsuke, Iwata|ja|磐田市見付}}, this shrine is the {{ill|sōja|ja|惣社}} of Tōtōmi Province and enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}. * '''{{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小国神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|ja|森町}}, this shrine is the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of Tōtōmi Province, enshrining {{ill|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴神}}. === Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Kochōshizuka Kofun|ja|小銚子塚古墳}}''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten,'' {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}. * Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan. * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社}}. * ''Daijiten of Shintō,'' {{ill|Rinsen Bookstore|ja|臨川書店}}. * ''General Overview of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] {{Japanese clan |Name = Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō |Crest = |Crest Name = |Main Surname = {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} |Founder = {{ill|Inagimi no Mikoto|ja|印岐美命}} |Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Tenson|ja|天孫}}) |Origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} |Base = Tōtsuōmi Province (later {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}) |Notable Figures = See {{section link|#Notable Figures}} |Branches = (Parentheses denote clan suffix)
{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}
{{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|久努国造}}
{{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久野氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kuji Kokuzō|ja|久自国造}}
{{ill|Ōbe clan|ja|大部氏}} (Zō) }} '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō''' (遠淡海国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Tōtsuōmi Province, which encompassed the western region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. The title is also rendered as Tōtōmi Kokuzō. == Overview == * The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} mentions the title as '''Tōtōmi Kokuzō'''. The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} refers to it as '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō'''. The progenitor of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō is recorded as {{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|Takehi-Nateru=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Takeruhiratori no Mikoto}}, a descendant of [[Ame no Hohi]], linking the lineage to the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|出雲国造}}, {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造|lt=Musashi Kokuzō}}, and other related clans. * The ''Kokuzō Hongi'' of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' names {{ill|Inagimi no Mikoto|ja|印岐美命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}}, as the Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' states that Inagimi no Mikoto, the brother of {{ill|Katakatane no Mikoto|ja|片堅石命}}, founder of {{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}, was appointed Kokuzō of Tōtsuōmi. This record also describes Inagimi as the ancestor of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō, and several related clans.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan belonged to the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}. While the clan's official {{ill|uji|ja|氏}} and {{ill|kabane|ja|姓}} remain uncertain, it is believed to have adopted '''Tōtsuōmi''' (遠淡海氏) as its name, with the kabane of '''Nao''' (直), like its related clans {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久努氏}} and {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}."[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/mononobe2g.htm Overview of the Mononobe Clan]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2003. The clan is also associated with the use of "Tachibana" (橘) and the mythical rejuvenating "Ochimizu" (変若水). Several descendants later adopted the {{ill|Tachibana no Ason|ja|橘朝臣}} surname.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/monobekz/monobek1.htm Examination of the Mononobe Lineage in the ''Tenson Hongi'']," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2008. == Headquarters == === Main Base === The headquarters of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō were in {{ill|Iwata District, Tōtōmi|ja|磐田郡}}, corresponding to modern {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市}}. This area later became the site of the provincial {{ill|kokufu|ja|国府}} and {{ill|Provincial temple|ja|国分寺}} of Tōtōmi Province. === Territory === The Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō governed the region known as Tōtsuōmi, later divided into Iwata District and surrounding areas, including {{ill|Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|ja|山香郡}}, {{ill|Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|ja|長上郡}}, and {{ill|Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|ja|長下郡}}. The boundaries of the Kokuzō's territory remain debated, with theories including: * Surroundings of {{ill|Lake Hamana|ja|浜名湖}}. * West of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. * From Lake Hamana to the western edge of {{ill|Iwata Plain|ja|磐田原}}. * From Lake Hamana to the {{ill|Ōtagawa River|ja|太田川}} basin. == Clan Deity == === Shrine Associations === * '''{{ill|Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|ja|淡海國玉神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mitsuke, Iwata|ja|磐田市見付}}, this shrine is the {{ill|sōja|ja|惣社}} of Tōtōmi Province and enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}. * '''{{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小国神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|ja|森町}}, this shrine is the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of Tōtōmi Province, enshrining {{ill|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴神}}. === Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kochōshizuka Kofun|ja|小銚子塚古墳}}''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten,'' {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}. * Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan. * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社}}. * ''Daijiten of Shintō,'' {{ill|Rinsen Bookstore|ja|臨川書店}}. * ''General Overview of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}} {{ill|Ōbe clan|ja|大部氏}} (Zō) }} '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō''' (遠淡海国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Tōtsuōmi Province, which encompassed the western region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. The title is also rendered as Tōtōmi Kokuzō. == Overview == * The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} mentions the title as '''Tōtōmi Kokuzō'''. The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} refers to it as '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō'''. The progenitor of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō is recorded as {{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|Takehi-Nateru=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Takeruhiratori no Mikoto}}, a descendant of [[Ame no Hohi]], linking the lineage to the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|出雲国造}}, {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造|lt=Musashi Kokuzō}}, and other related clans. * The ''Kokuzō Hongi'' of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' names {{ill|Inagimi no Mikoto|ja|印岐美命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}}, as the Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' states that Inagimi no Mikoto, the brother of {{ill|Katakatane no Mikoto|ja|片堅石命}}, founder of {{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}, was appointed Kokuzō of Tōtsuōmi. This record also describes Inagimi as the ancestor of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō, and several related clans.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan belonged to the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}. While the clan's official {{ill|uji|ja|氏}} and {{ill|kabane|ja|姓}} remain uncertain, it is believed to have adopted '''Tōtsuōmi''' (遠淡海氏) as its name, with the kabane of '''Nao''' (直), like its related clans {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久努氏}} and {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}."[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/mononobe2g.htm Overview of the Mononobe Clan]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2003. The clan is also associated with the use of "Tachibana" (橘) and the mythical rejuvenating "Ochimizu" (変若水). Several descendants later adopted the {{ill|Tachibana no Ason|ja|橘朝臣}} surname.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/monobekz/monobek1.htm Examination of the Mononobe Lineage in the ''Tenson Hongi'']," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2008. == Headquarters == === Main Base === The headquarters of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō were in {{ill|Iwata District, Tōtōmi|ja|磐田郡}}, corresponding to modern {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市}}. This area later became the site of the provincial {{ill|kokufu|ja|国府}} and {{ill|Provincial temple|ja|国分寺}} of Tōtōmi Province. === Territory === The Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō governed the region known as Tōtsuōmi, later divided into Iwata District and surrounding areas, including {{ill|Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|ja|山香郡}}, {{ill|Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|ja|長上郡}}, and {{ill|Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|ja|長下郡}}. The boundaries of the Kokuzō's territory remain debated, with theories including: * Surroundings of {{ill|Lake Hamana|ja|浜名湖}}. * West of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. * From Lake Hamana to the western edge of {{ill|Iwata Plain|ja|磐田原}}. * From Lake Hamana to the {{ill|Ōtagawa River|ja|太田川}} basin. == Clan Deity == === Shrine Associations === * '''{{ill|Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|ja|淡海國玉神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mitsuke, Iwata|ja|磐田市見付}}, this shrine is the {{ill|sōja|ja|惣社}} of Tōtōmi Province and enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}. * '''{{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小国神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|ja|森町}}, this shrine is the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of Tōtōmi Province, enshrining {{ill|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴神}}. === Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kochōshizuka Kofun|ja|小銚子塚古墳}}''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten,'' {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}. * Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan. * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社}}. * ''Daijiten of Shintō,'' {{ill|Rinsen Bookstore|ja|臨川書店}}. * ''General Overview of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}} The progenitor of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō is recorded as {{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|Takehi-Nateru=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Takeruhiratori no Mikoto}}, a descendant of [[Ame no Hohi]], linking the lineage to the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|出雲国造}}, {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造|lt=Musashi Kokuzō}}, and other related clans. * The ''Kokuzō Hongi'' of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' names {{ill|Inagimi no Mikoto|ja|印岐美命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}}, as the Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' states that Inagimi no Mikoto, the brother of {{ill|Katakatane no Mikoto|ja|片堅石命}}, founder of {{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}, was appointed Kokuzō of Tōtsuōmi. This record also describes Inagimi as the ancestor of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō, and several related clans.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan belonged to the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}. While the clan's official {{ill|uji|ja|氏}} and {{ill|kabane|ja|姓}} remain uncertain, it is believed to have adopted '''Tōtsuōmi''' (遠淡海氏) as its name, with the kabane of '''Nao''' (直), like its related clans {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久努氏}} and {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}."[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/mononobe2g.htm Overview of the Mononobe Clan]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2003. The clan is also associated with the use of "Tachibana" (橘) and the mythical rejuvenating "Ochimizu" (変若水). Several descendants later adopted the {{ill|Tachibana no Ason|ja|橘朝臣}} surname.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/monobekz/monobek1.htm Examination of the Mononobe Lineage in the ''Tenson Hongi'']," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2008. == Headquarters == === Main Base === The headquarters of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō were in {{ill|Iwata District, Tōtōmi|ja|磐田郡}}, corresponding to modern {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市}}. This area later became the site of the provincial {{ill|kokufu|ja|国府}} and {{ill|Provincial temple|ja|国分寺}} of Tōtōmi Province. === Territory === The Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō governed the region known as Tōtsuōmi, later divided into Iwata District and surrounding areas, including {{ill|Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|ja|山香郡}}, {{ill|Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|ja|長上郡}}, and {{ill|Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|ja|長下郡}}. The boundaries of the Kokuzō's territory remain debated, with theories including: * Surroundings of {{ill|Lake Hamana|ja|浜名湖}}. * West of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. * From Lake Hamana to the western edge of {{ill|Iwata Plain|ja|磐田原}}. * From Lake Hamana to the {{ill|Ōtagawa River|ja|太田川}} basin. == Clan Deity == === Shrine Associations === * '''{{ill|Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|ja|淡海國玉神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mitsuke, Iwata|ja|磐田市見付}}, this shrine is the {{ill|sōja|ja|惣社}} of Tōtōmi Province and enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}. * '''{{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小国神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|ja|森町}}, this shrine is the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of Tōtōmi Province, enshrining {{ill|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴神}}. === Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kochōshizuka Kofun|ja|小銚子塚古墳}}''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten,'' {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}. * Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan. * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社}}. * ''Daijiten of Shintō,'' {{ill|Rinsen Bookstore|ja|臨川書店}}. * ''General Overview of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}} {{ill|Kuji Kokuzō|ja|久自国造}}
{{ill|Ōbe clan|ja|大部氏}} (Zō) }} '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō''' (遠淡海国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Tōtsuōmi Province, which encompassed the western region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. The title is also rendered as Tōtōmi Kokuzō. == Overview == * The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} mentions the title as '''Tōtōmi Kokuzō'''. The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} refers to it as '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō'''. The progenitor of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō is recorded as {{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|Takehi-Nateru=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Takeruhiratori no Mikoto}}, a descendant of [[Ame no Hohi]], linking the lineage to the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|出雲国造}}, {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造|lt=Musashi Kokuzō}}, and other related clans. * The ''Kokuzō Hongi'' of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' names {{ill|Inagimi no Mikoto|ja|印岐美命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}}, as the Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' states that Inagimi no Mikoto, the brother of {{ill|Katakatane no Mikoto|ja|片堅石命}}, founder of {{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}, was appointed Kokuzō of Tōtsuōmi. This record also describes Inagimi as the ancestor of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō, and several related clans.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan belonged to the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}. While the clan's official {{ill|uji|ja|氏}} and {{ill|kabane|ja|姓}} remain uncertain, it is believed to have adopted '''Tōtsuōmi''' (遠淡海氏) as its name, with the kabane of '''Nao''' (直), like its related clans {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久努氏}} and {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}."[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/mononobe2g.htm Overview of the Mononobe Clan]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2003. The clan is also associated with the use of "Tachibana" (橘) and the mythical rejuvenating "Ochimizu" (変若水). Several descendants later adopted the {{ill|Tachibana no Ason|ja|橘朝臣}} surname.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/monobekz/monobek1.htm Examination of the Mononobe Lineage in the ''Tenson Hongi'']," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2008. == Headquarters == === Main Base === The headquarters of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō were in {{ill|Iwata District, Tōtōmi|ja|磐田郡}}, corresponding to modern {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市}}. This area later became the site of the provincial {{ill|kokufu|ja|国府}} and {{ill|Provincial temple|ja|国分寺}} of Tōtōmi Province. === Territory === The Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō governed the region known as Tōtsuōmi, later divided into Iwata District and surrounding areas, including {{ill|Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|ja|山香郡}}, {{ill|Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|ja|長上郡}}, and {{ill|Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|ja|長下郡}}. The boundaries of the Kokuzō's territory remain debated, with theories including: * Surroundings of {{ill|Lake Hamana|ja|浜名湖}}. * West of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. * From Lake Hamana to the western edge of {{ill|Iwata Plain|ja|磐田原}}. * From Lake Hamana to the {{ill|Ōtagawa River|ja|太田川}} basin. == Clan Deity == === Shrine Associations === * '''{{ill|Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|ja|淡海國玉神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mitsuke, Iwata|ja|磐田市見付}}, this shrine is the {{ill|sōja|ja|惣社}} of Tōtōmi Province and enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}. * '''{{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小国神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|ja|森町}}, this shrine is the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of Tōtōmi Province, enshrining {{ill|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴神}}. === Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kochōshizuka Kofun|ja|小銚子塚古墳}}''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten,'' {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}. * Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan. * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社}}. * ''Daijiten of Shintō,'' {{ill|Rinsen Bookstore|ja|臨川書店}}. * The boundaries of the Kokuzō's territory remain debated, with theories including: * Surroundings of {{ill|Lake Hamana|ja|浜名湖}}. * West of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. * From Lake Hamana to the western edge of {{ill|Iwata Plain|ja|磐田原}}. * From Lake Hamana to the {{ill|Ōtagawa River|ja|太田川}} basin. == Clan Deity == === Shrine Associations === * '''{{ill|Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|ja|淡海國玉神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mitsuke, Iwata|ja|磐田市見付}}, this shrine is the {{ill|sōja|ja|惣社}} of Tōtōmi Province and enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}. * '''{{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小国神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|ja|森町}}, this shrine is the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of Tōtōmi Province, enshrining {{ill|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴神}}. === Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kochōshizuka Kofun|ja|小銚子塚古墳}}''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten,'' {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}. * Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio, ''Dictionary {{ill|Ōbe clan|ja|大部氏}} (Zō) }} '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō''' (遠淡海国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Tōtsuōmi Province, which encompassed the western region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. The title is also rendered as Tōtōmi Kokuzō. == Overview == * The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} mentions the title as '''Tōtōmi Kokuzō'''. The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} refers to it as '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō'''. The progenitor of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō is recorded as {{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|Takehi-Nateru=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Takeruhiratori no Mikoto}}, a descendant of [[Ame no Hohi]], linking the lineage to the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|出雲国造}}, {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造|lt=Musashi Kokuzō}}, and other related clans. * The ''Kokuzō Hongi'' of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' names {{ill|Inagimi no Mikoto|ja|印岐美命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}}, as the Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' states that Inagimi no Mikoto, the brother of {{ill|Katakatane no Mikoto|ja|片堅石命}}, founder of {{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}, was appointed Kokuzō of Tōtsuōmi. This record also describes Inagimi as the ancestor of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō, and several related clans.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan belonged to the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}. While the clan's official {{ill|uji|ja|氏}} and {{ill|kabane|ja|姓}} remain uncertain, it is believed to have adopted '''Tōtsuōmi''' (遠淡海氏) as its name, with the kabane of '''Nao''' (直), like its related clans {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久努氏}} and {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}."[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/mononobe2g.htm Overview of the Mononobe Clan]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2003. The clan is also associated with the use of "Tachibana" (橘) and the mythical rejuvenating "Ochimizu" (変若水). Several descendants later adopted the {{ill|Tachibana no Ason|ja|橘朝臣}} surname.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/monobekz/monobek1.htm Examination of the Mononobe Lineage in the ''Tenson Hongi'']," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2008. == Headquarters == === Main Base === The headquarters of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō were in {{ill|Iwata District, Tōtōmi|ja|磐田郡}}, corresponding to modern {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市}}. This area later became the site of the provincial {{ill|kokufu|ja|国府}} and {{ill|Provincial temple|ja|国分寺}} of Tōtōmi Province. === Territory === The Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō governed the region known as Tōtsuōmi, later divided into Iwata District and surrounding areas, including {{ill|Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|ja|山香郡}}, {{ill|Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|ja|長上郡}}, and {{ill|Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|ja|長下郡}}. The boundaries of the Kokuzō's territory remain debated, with theories including: * Surroundings of {{ill|Lake Hamana|ja|浜名湖}}. * West of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. * From Lake Hamana to the western edge of {{ill|Iwata Plain|ja|磐田原}}. * From Lake Hamana to the {{ill|Ōtagawa River|ja|太田川}} basin. == Clan Deity == === Shrine Associations === * '''{{ill|Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|ja|淡海國玉神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mitsuke, Iwata|ja|磐田市見付}}, this shrine is the {{ill|sōja|ja|惣社}} of Tōtōmi Province and enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}. * '''{{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小国神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|ja|森町}}, this shrine is the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of Tōtōmi Province, enshrining {{ill|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴神}}. === Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kochōshizuka Kofun|ja|小銚子塚古墳}}''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten,'' {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}. * Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan. * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社}}. * ''Daijiten of Shintō,'' {{ill|Rinsen Bookstore|ja|臨川書店}}. * This area later became the site of the provincial {{ill|kokufu|ja|国府}} and {{ill|Provincial temple|ja|国分寺}} of Tōtōmi Province. === Territory === The Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō governed the region known as Tōtsuōmi, later divided into Iwata District and surrounding areas, including {{ill|Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|ja|山香郡}}, {{ill|Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|ja|長上郡}}, and {{ill|Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|ja|長下郡}}. The boundaries of the Kokuzō's territory remain debated, with theories including: * Surroundings of {{ill|Lake Hamana|ja|浜名湖}}. * West of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. * From Lake Hamana to the western edge of {{ill|Iwata Plain|ja|磐田原}}. * From Lake Hamana to the {{ill|Ōtagawa River|ja|太田川}} basin. == Clan Deity == === Shrine Associations === * '''{{ill|Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|ja|淡海國玉神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mitsuke, Iwata|ja|磐田市見付}}, this shrine is the {{ill|sōja|ja|惣社}} of Tōtōmi Province and enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}. * '''{{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小国神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|ja|森町}}, this shrine is the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of Tōtōmi Province, enshrining {{ill|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴神}}. === Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kochōshizuka Kofun|ja|小銚子塚古墳}}''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten,'' {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}. * Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio, ''Dictionary {{ill|Ōbe clan|ja|大部氏}} (Zō) }} '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō''' (遠淡海国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Tōtsuōmi Province, which encompassed the western region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. The title is also rendered as Tōtōmi Kokuzō. == Overview == * The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} mentions the title as '''Tōtōmi Kokuzō'''. The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} refers to it as '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō'''. The progenitor of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō is recorded as {{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|Takehi-Nateru=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Takeruhiratori no Mikoto}}, a descendant of [[Ame no Hohi]], linking the lineage to the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|出雲国造}}, {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造|lt=Musashi Kokuzō}}, and other related clans. * The ''Kokuzō Hongi'' of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' names {{ill|Inagimi no Mikoto|ja|印岐美命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}}, as the Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' states that Inagimi no Mikoto, the brother of {{ill|Katakatane no Mikoto|ja|片堅石命}}, founder of {{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}, was appointed Kokuzō of Tōtsuōmi. This record also describes Inagimi as the ancestor of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō, and several related clans.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan belonged to the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}. While the clan's official {{ill|uji|ja|氏}} and {{ill|kabane|ja|姓}} remain uncertain, it is believed to have adopted '''Tōtsuōmi''' (遠淡海氏) as its name, with the kabane of '''Nao''' (直), like its related clans {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久努氏}} and {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}."[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/mononobe2g.htm Overview of the Mononobe Clan]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2003. The clan is also associated with the use of "Tachibana" (橘) and the mythical rejuvenating "Ochimizu" (変若水). Several descendants later adopted the {{ill|Tachibana no Ason|ja|橘朝臣}} surname.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/monobekz/monobek1.htm Examination of the Mononobe Lineage in the ''Tenson Hongi'']," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2008. == Headquarters == === Main Base === The headquarters of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō were in {{ill|Iwata District, Tōtōmi|ja|磐田郡}}, corresponding to modern {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市}}. This area later became the site of the provincial {{ill|kokufu|ja|国府}} and {{ill|Provincial temple|ja|国分寺}} of Tōtōmi Province. === Territory === The Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō governed the region known as Tōtsuōmi, later divided into Iwata District and surrounding areas, including {{ill|Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|ja|山香郡}}, {{ill|Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|ja|長上郡}}, and {{ill|Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|ja|長下郡}}. The boundaries of the Kokuzō's territory remain debated, with theories including: * Surroundings of {{ill|Lake Hamana|ja|浜名湖}}. * West of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. * From Lake Hamana to the western edge of {{ill|Iwata Plain|ja|磐田原}}. * From Lake Hamana to the {{ill|Ōtagawa River|ja|太田川}} basin. == Clan Deity == === Shrine Associations === * '''{{ill|Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|ja|淡海國玉神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mitsuke, Iwata|ja|磐田市見付}}, this shrine is the {{ill|sōja|ja|惣社}} of Tōtōmi Province and enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}. * '''{{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小国神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|ja|森町}}, this shrine is the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of Tōtōmi Province, enshrining {{ill|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴神}}. === Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kochōshizuka Kofun|ja|小銚子塚古墳}}''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten,'' {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}. * Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan. * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社}}. * ''Daijiten of Shintō,'' {{ill|Rinsen Bookstore|ja|臨川書店}}. * western edge of {{ill|Iwata Plain|ja|磐田原}}. * From Lake Hamana to the {{ill|Ōtagawa River|ja|太田川}} basin. == Clan Deity == === Shrine Associations === * '''{{ill|Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|ja|淡海國玉神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mitsuke, Iwata|ja|磐田市見付}}, this shrine is the {{ill|sōja|ja|惣社}} of Tōtōmi Province and enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}. * '''{{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小国神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|ja|森町}}, this shrine is the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of Tōtōmi Province, enshrining {{ill|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴神}}. === Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kochōshizuka Kofun|ja|小銚子塚古墳}}''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten,'' {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}. * Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio, ''Dictionary {{Japanese clan |Name = Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō |Crest = |Crest Name = |Main Surname = {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} |Founder = {{ill|Inagimi no Mikoto|ja|印岐美命}} |Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Tenson|ja|天孫}}) |Origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} |Base = Tōtsuōmi Province (later {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}) |Notable Figures = See {{section link|#Notable Figures}} |Branches = (Parentheses denote clan suffix)
{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}
{{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|久努国造}}
{{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久野氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao)

{{ill|Ōbe clan|ja|大部氏}} (Zō) }} '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō''' (遠淡海国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Tōtsuōmi Province, which encompassed the western region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. The title is also rendered as Tōtōmi Kokuzō. == Overview == * The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} mentions the title as '''Tōtōmi Kokuzō'''. The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} refers to it as '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō'''. The progenitor of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō is recorded as {{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|Takehi-Nateru=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Takeruhiratori no Mikoto}}, a descendant of [[Ame no Hohi]], linking the lineage to the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|出雲国造}}, {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造|lt=Musashi Kokuzō}}, and other related clans. * The ''Kokuzō Hongi'' of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' names {{ill|Inagimi no Mikoto|ja|印岐美命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}}, as the Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' states that Inagimi no Mikoto, the brother of {{ill|Katakatane no Mikoto|ja|片堅石命}}, founder of {{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}, was appointed Kokuzō of Tōtsuōmi. This record also describes Inagimi as the ancestor of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō, and several related clans.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan belonged to the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}. While the clan's official {{ill|uji|ja|氏}} and {{ill|kabane|ja|姓}} remain uncertain, it is believed to have adopted '''Tōtsuōmi''' (遠淡海氏) as its name, with the kabane of '''Nao''' (直), like its related clans {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久努氏}} and {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}."[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/mononobe2g.htm Overview of the Mononobe Clan]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2003. The clan is also associated with the use of "Tachibana" (橘) and the mythical rejuvenating "Ochimizu" (変若水). Several descendants later adopted the {{ill|Tachibana no Ason|ja|橘朝臣}} surname.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/monobekz/monobek1.htm Examination of the Mononobe Lineage in the ''Tenson Hongi'']," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2008. == Headquarters == === Main Base === The headquarters of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō were in {{ill|Iwata District, Tōtōmi|ja|磐田郡}}, corresponding to modern {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市}}. This area later became the site of the provincial {{ill|kokufu|ja|国府}} and {{ill|Provincial temple|ja|国分寺}} of Tōtōmi Province. === Territory === The Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō governed the region known as Tōtsuōmi, later divided into Iwata District and surrounding areas, including {{ill|Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|ja|山香郡}}, {{ill|Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|ja|長上郡}}, and {{ill|Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|ja|長下郡}}. The boundaries of the Kokuzō's territory remain debated, with theories including: * Surroundings of {{ill|Lake Hamana|ja|浜名湖}}. * West of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. * From Lake Hamana to the western edge of {{ill|Iwata Plain|ja|磐田原}}. * From Lake Hamana to the {{ill|Ōtagawa River|ja|太田川}} basin. == Clan Deity == === Shrine Associations === * '''{{ill|Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|ja|淡海國玉神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mitsuke, Iwata|ja|磐田市見付}}, this shrine is the {{ill|sōja|ja|惣社}} of Tōtōmi Province and enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}. * '''{{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小国神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|ja|森町}}, this shrine is the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of Tōtōmi Province, enshrining {{ill|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴神}}. === Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kochōshizuka Kofun|ja|小銚子塚古墳}}''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten,'' {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}. * Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan. * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社}}. * ''Daijiten of Shintō,'' {{ill|Rinsen Bookstore|ja|臨川書店}}. * ''General Overview of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}} Located in {{ill|Mitsuke, Iwata|ja|磐田市見付}}, this shrine is the {{ill|sōja|ja|惣社}} of Tōtōmi Province and enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}. * '''{{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小国神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|ja|森町}}, this shrine is the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of Tōtōmi Province, enshrining {{ill|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴神}}. === Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka {{Japanese clan |Name = Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō |Crest = |Crest Name = |Main Surname = {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} |Founder = {{ill|Inagimi no Mikoto|ja|印岐美命}} |Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Tenson|ja|天孫}}) |Origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} |Base = Tōtsuōmi Province (later {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}) |Notable Figures = See {{section link|#Notable Figures}} |Branches = (Parentheses denote clan suffix)
{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}
{{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|久努国造}}
{{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久野氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kuji Kokuzō|ja|久自国造}}
{{ill|Ōbe clan|ja|大部氏}} (Zō) }} '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō''' (遠淡海国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Tōtsuōmi Province, which encompassed the western region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. The title is also rendered as Tōtōmi Kokuzō. == Overview == * The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} mentions the title as '''Tōtōmi Kokuzō'''. The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} refers to it as '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō'''. The progenitor of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō is recorded as {{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|Takehi-Nateru=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Takeruhiratori no Mikoto}}, a descendant of [[Ame no Hohi]], linking the lineage to the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|出雲国造}}, {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造|lt=Musashi Kokuzō}}, and other related clans. * The ''Kokuzō Hongi'' of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' names {{ill|Inagimi no Mikoto|ja|印岐美命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}}, as the Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' states that Inagimi no Mikoto, the brother of {{ill|Katakatane no Mikoto|ja|片堅石命}}, founder of {{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}, was appointed Kokuzō of Tōtsuōmi. This record also describes Inagimi as the ancestor of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō, and several related clans.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan belonged to the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}. While the clan's official {{ill|uji|ja|氏}} and {{ill|kabane|ja|姓}} remain uncertain, it is believed to have adopted '''Tōtsuōmi''' (遠淡海氏) as its name, with the kabane of '''Nao''' (直), like its related clans {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久努氏}} and {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}."[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/mononobe2g.htm Overview of the Mononobe Clan]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2003. The clan is also associated with the use of "Tachibana" (橘) and the mythical rejuvenating "Ochimizu" (変若水). Several descendants later adopted the {{ill|Tachibana no Ason|ja|橘朝臣}} surname.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/monobekz/monobek1.htm Examination of the Mononobe Lineage in the ''Tenson Hongi'']," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2008. == Headquarters == === Main Base === The headquarters of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō were in {{ill|Iwata District, Tōtōmi|ja|磐田郡}}, corresponding to modern {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市}}. This area later became the site of the provincial {{ill|kokufu|ja|国府}} and {{ill|Provincial temple|ja|国分寺}} of Tōtōmi Province. === Territory === The Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō governed the region known as Tōtsuōmi, later divided into Iwata District and surrounding areas, including {{ill|Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|ja|山香郡}}, {{ill|Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|ja|長上郡}}, and {{ill|Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|ja|長下郡}}. The boundaries of the Kokuzō's territory remain debated, with theories including: * Surroundings of {{ill|Lake Hamana|ja|浜名湖}}. * West of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. * From Lake Hamana to the western edge of {{ill|Iwata Plain|ja|磐田原}}. * From Lake Hamana to the {{ill|Ōtagawa River|ja|太田川}} basin. == Clan Deity == === Shrine Associations === * '''{{ill|Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|ja|淡海國玉神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mitsuke, Iwata|ja|磐田市見付}}, this shrine is the {{ill|sōja|ja|惣社}} of Tōtōmi Province and enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}. * '''{{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小国神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|ja|森町}}, this shrine is the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of Tōtōmi Province, enshrining {{ill|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴神}}. === Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kochōshizuka Kofun|ja|小銚子塚古墳}}''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten,'' {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}. * Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan. * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社}}. * ''Daijiten of Shintō,'' {{ill|Rinsen Bookstore|ja|臨川書店}}. * ''General Overview of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}} Located in {{ill|Mitsuke, Iwata|ja|磐田市見付}}, this shrine is the {{ill|sōja|ja|惣社}} of Tōtōmi Province and enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}. * '''{{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小国神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|ja|森町}}, this shrine is the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of Tōtōmi Province, enshrining {{ill|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴神}}. === Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kochōshizuka Kofun|ja|小銚子塚古墳}}''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten,'' {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}. * Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan. * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社}}. * ''Daijiten of Shintō,'' {{ill|Rinsen Bookstore|ja|臨川書店}}. * ''General Overview of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}} {{Japanese clan |Name = Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō |Crest = |Crest Name = |Main Surname = {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} |Founder = {{ill|Inagimi no Mikoto|ja|印岐美命}} |Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Tenson|ja|天孫}}) |Origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} |Base = Tōtsuōmi Province (later {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}) |Notable Figures = See {{section link|#Notable Figures}} |Branches = (Parentheses denote clan suffix)
{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}
{{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|久努国造}}
{{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久野氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kuji Kokuzō|ja|久自国造}}
{{ill|Ōbe clan|ja|大部氏}} (Zō) }} '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō''' (遠淡海国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Tōtsuōmi Province, which encompassed the western region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. The title is also rendered as Tōtōmi Kokuzō. == Overview == * The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} mentions the title as '''Tōtōmi Kokuzō'''. The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} refers to it as '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō'''. The progenitor of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō is recorded as {{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|Takehi-Nateru=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Takeruhiratori no Mikoto}}, a descendant of [[Ame no Hohi]], linking the lineage to the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|出雲国造}}, {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造|lt=Musashi Kokuzō}}, and other related clans. * The ''Kokuzō Hongi'' of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' names {{ill|Inagimi no Mikoto|ja|印岐美命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}}, as the Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' states that Inagimi no Mikoto, the brother of {{ill|Katakatane no Mikoto|ja|片堅石命}}, founder of {{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}, was appointed Kokuzō of Tōtsuōmi. This record also describes Inagimi as the ancestor of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō, and several related clans.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan belonged to the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}. While the clan's official {{ill|uji|ja|氏}} and {{ill|kabane|ja|姓}} remain uncertain, it is believed to have adopted '''Tōtsuōmi''' (遠淡海氏) as its name, with the kabane of '''Nao''' (直), like its related clans {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久努氏}} and {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}."[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/mononobe2g.htm Overview of the Mononobe Clan]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2003. The clan is also associated with the use of "Tachibana" (橘) and the mythical rejuvenating "Ochimizu" (変若水). Several descendants later adopted the {{ill|Tachibana no Ason|ja|橘朝臣}} surname.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/monobekz/monobek1.htm Examination of the Mononobe Lineage in the ''Tenson Hongi'']," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2008. == Headquarters == === Main Base === The headquarters of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō were in {{ill|Iwata District, Tōtōmi|ja|磐田郡}}, corresponding to modern {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市}}. This area later became the site of the provincial {{ill|kokufu|ja|国府}} and {{ill|Provincial temple|ja|国分寺}} of Tōtōmi Province. === Territory === The Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō governed the region known as Tōtsuōmi, later divided into Iwata District and surrounding areas, including {{ill|Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|ja|山香郡}}, {{ill|Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|ja|長上郡}}, and {{ill|Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|ja|長下郡}}. The boundaries of the Kokuzō's territory remain debated, with theories including: * Surroundings of {{ill|Lake Hamana|ja|浜名湖}}. * West of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. * From Lake Hamana to the western edge of {{ill|Iwata Plain|ja|磐田原}}. * From Lake Hamana to the {{ill|Ōtagawa River|ja|太田川}} basin. == Clan Deity == === Shrine Associations === * '''{{ill|Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|ja|淡海國玉神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mitsuke, Iwata|ja|磐田市見付}}, this shrine is the {{ill|sōja|ja|惣社}} of Tōtōmi Province and enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}. * '''{{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小国神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|ja|森町}}, this shrine is the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of Tōtōmi Province, enshrining {{ill|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴神}}. === Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kochōshizuka Kofun|ja|小銚子塚古墳}}''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten,'' {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}. * Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan. * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社}}. * ''Daijiten of Shintō,'' {{ill|Rinsen Bookstore|ja|臨川書店}}. * ''General Overview of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}} western edge of {{ill|Iwata Plain|ja|磐田原}}. * From Lake Hamana to the {{ill|Ōtagawa River|ja|太田川}} basin. == Clan Deity == === Shrine Associations === * '''{{ill|Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|ja|淡海國玉神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mitsuke, Iwata|ja|磐田市見付}}, this shrine is the {{ill|sōja|ja|惣社}} of Tōtōmi Province and enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}. * '''{{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小国神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|ja|森町}}, this shrine is the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of Tōtōmi Province, enshrining {{ill|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴神}}. === Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kochōshizuka Kofun|ja|小銚子塚古墳}}''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten,'' {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}. * Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio, ''Dictionary {{Japanese clan |Name = Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō |Crest = |Crest Name = |Main Surname = {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} |Founder = {{ill|Inagimi no Mikoto|ja|印岐美命}} |Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Tenson|ja|天孫}}) |Origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} |Base = Tōtsuōmi Province (later {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}) |Notable Figures = See {{section link|#Notable Figures}} |Branches = (Parentheses denote clan suffix)
{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}
{{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|久努国造}}
{{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久野氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}} (Nao)

{{ill|Ōbe clan|ja|大部氏}} (Zō) }} '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō''' (遠淡海国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Tōtsuōmi Province, which encompassed the western region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. The title is also rendered as Tōtōmi Kokuzō. == Overview == * The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} mentions the title as '''Tōtōmi Kokuzō'''. The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} refers to it as '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō'''. The progenitor of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō is recorded as {{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|Takehi-Nateru=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Takeruhiratori no Mikoto}}, a descendant of [[Ame no Hohi]], linking the lineage to the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|出雲国造}}, {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造|lt=Musashi Kokuzō}}, and other related clans. * The ''Kokuzō Hongi'' of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' names {{ill|Inagimi no Mikoto|ja|印岐美命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}}, as the Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' states that Inagimi no Mikoto, the brother of {{ill|Katakatane no Mikoto|ja|片堅石命}}, founder of {{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}, was appointed Kokuzō of Tōtsuōmi. This record also describes Inagimi as the ancestor of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō, and several related clans.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan belonged to the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}. While the clan's official {{ill|uji|ja|氏}} and {{ill|kabane|ja|姓}} remain uncertain, it is believed to have adopted '''Tōtsuōmi''' (遠淡海氏) as its name, with the kabane of '''Nao''' (直), like its related clans {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久努氏}} and {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}."[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/mononobe2g.htm Overview of the Mononobe Clan]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2003. The clan is also associated with the use of "Tachibana" (橘) and the mythical rejuvenating "Ochimizu" (変若水). Several descendants later adopted the {{ill|Tachibana no Ason|ja|橘朝臣}} surname.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/monobekz/monobek1.htm Examination of the Mononobe Lineage in the ''Tenson Hongi'']," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2008. == Headquarters == === Main Base === The headquarters of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō were in {{ill|Iwata District, Tōtōmi|ja|磐田郡}}, corresponding to modern {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市}}. This area later became the site of the provincial {{ill|kokufu|ja|国府}} and {{ill|Provincial temple|ja|国分寺}} of Tōtōmi Province. === Territory === The Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō governed the region known as Tōtsuōmi, later divided into Iwata District and surrounding areas, including {{ill|Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|ja|山香郡}}, {{ill|Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|ja|長上郡}}, and {{ill|Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|ja|長下郡}}. The boundaries of the Kokuzō's territory remain debated, with theories including: * Surroundings of {{ill|Lake Hamana|ja|浜名湖}}. * West of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. * From Lake Hamana to the western edge of {{ill|Iwata Plain|ja|磐田原}}. * From Lake Hamana to the {{ill|Ōtagawa River|ja|太田川}} basin. == Clan Deity == === Shrine Associations === * '''{{ill|Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|ja|淡海國玉神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mitsuke, Iwata|ja|磐田市見付}}, this shrine is the {{ill|sōja|ja|惣社}} of Tōtōmi Province and enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}. * '''{{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小国神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|ja|森町}}, this shrine is the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of Tōtōmi Province, enshrining {{ill|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴神}}. === Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kochōshizuka Kofun|ja|小銚子塚古墳}}''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten,'' {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}. * Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan. * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社}}. * ''Daijiten of Shintō,'' {{ill|Rinsen Bookstore|ja|臨川書店}}. * ''General Overview of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}} Located in {{ill|Mitsuke, Iwata|ja|磐田市見付}}, this shrine is the {{ill|sōja|ja|惣社}} of Tōtōmi Province and enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}. * '''{{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小国神社}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|ja|森町}}, this shrine is the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of Tōtōmi Province, enshrining {{ill|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴神}}. === Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kochōshizuka Kofun|ja|小銚子塚古墳}}''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten,'' {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}. * Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan. * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社}}. * ''Daijiten of Shintō,'' {{ill|Rinsen Bookstore|ja|臨川書店}}. * ''General Overview of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}}
