{{ill|Ōbe clan|ja|大部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbe clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Zō) }} '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō''' (遠淡海国造) refers to the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kokuzō]] of Tōtsuōmi Province, which encompassed the western region of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]. The title is also rendered as Tōtōmi Kokuzō. == Overview == * The [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] mentions the title as '''Tōtōmi Kokuzō'''. The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] refers to it as '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō'''. The progenitor of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō is recorded as {{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|Takehi-Nateru=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Takeruhiratori no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}}, a descendant of [[Ame no Hohi]], linking the lineage to the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|Izumo Province 造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造|lt=Musashi Kokuzō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 无邪志国造}}, and other related clans. * The ''Kokuzō Hongi'' of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' names {{ill|Inagimi no Mikoto|ja|印岐美命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagimi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikaga Shikoo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o命|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55536799}}, as the Kokuzō during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' states that Inagimi no Mikoto, the brother of {{ill|Katakatane no Mikoto|ja|片堅石命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakatane no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, founder of {{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suruga Kokuzō|qq=|1=Suruga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11573348}}, was appointed Kokuzō of Tōtsuōmi. This record also describes Inagimi as the ancestor of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Agatanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō, and several related clans.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan belonged to the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]]. While the clan's official [[:en:Uji (clan)|Uji]] and {{ill|kabane|ja|姓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kabane|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} remain uncertain, it is believed to have adopted '''Tōtsuōmi''' (遠淡海氏) as its name, with the kabane of '''Nao''' (直), like its related clans {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久努氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/mononobe2g.htm Overview of the Mononobe Clan]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2003. The clan is also associated with the use of "Tachibana" (橘) and the mythical rejuvenating "Ochimizu" (変若水). Several descendants later adopted the {{ill|Tachibana no Ason|ja|橘朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Ason|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} surname.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/monobekz/monobek1.htm Examination of the Mononobe Lineage in the ''Tenson Hongi'']," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2008. == Headquarters == === Main Base === The headquarters of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō were in [[:en:Iwata District, Shizuoka|Iwata District, Tōtōmi]], corresponding to modern [[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata, Shizuoka]]. This area later became the site of the provincial [[:en:Kokufu|kokufu]] and [[:en:Provincial temple|Provincial temple]] of Tōtōmi Province. === Territory === The Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō governed the region known as Tōtsuōmi, later divided into Iwata District and surrounding areas, including {{ill|Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|ja|山香郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|ja|長上郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|qq=|1=Nagakami district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651399}}, and {{ill|Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|ja|長下郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The boundaries of the Kokuzō's territory remain debated, with theories including: * Surroundings of [[:en:Lake Hamana|Lake Hamana]]. * West of the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryū River]]. * From Lake Hamana to the western edge of {{ill|Iwata Plain|ja|磐田原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwata Plain|qq=|1=Iwatahara plateau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106982104}}. * From Lake Hamana to the [[:en:Ōta River|Ōtagawa River]] basin. == Clan Deity == === Shrine Associations === * '''{{ill|Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|ja|淡海國玉神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|qq=}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mitsuke, Iwata|ja|磐田市見付|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke, Iwata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this shrine is the {{ill|sōja|ja|惣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sōja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 総社|1=sōja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1107129}} of Tōtōmi Province and enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. * '''[[:en:Okuni Shrine|Oguni Shrine]]''' : Located in {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|ja|森町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori, Shizuoka|qq=|1=Morimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q854613}}, this shrine is the [[:en:Ichinomiya|Ichinomiya]] of Tōtōmi Province, enshrining {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. === Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kochōshizuka Kofun|ja|小銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 銚子塚古墳 (磐田市)}}''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten,'' {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}. * Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan. * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Publishing|qq=|1=Yamakawa Shuppansha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11468274}}. * ''Daijiten of Shintō,'' {{ill|Rinsen Bookstore|ja|臨川書店|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinsen Bookstore|qq=|1=Rinsen Shoten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612454}}. * {{ill|Ōbe clan|ja|大部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbe clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Zō) }} '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō''' (遠淡海国造) refers to the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kokuzō]] of Tōtsuōmi Province, which encompassed the western region of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]. The title is also rendered as Tōtōmi Kokuzō. == Overview == * The [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] mentions the title as '''Tōtōmi Kokuzō'''. The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] refers to it as '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō'''. The progenitor of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō is recorded as {{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|Takehi-Nateru=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Takeruhiratori no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}}, a descendant of [[Ame no Hohi]], linking the lineage to the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|Izumo Province 造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造|lt=Musashi Kokuzō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 无邪志国造}}, and other related clans. * The ''Kokuzō Hongi'' of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' names {{ill|Inagimi no Mikoto|ja|印岐美命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagimi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikaga Shikoo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o命|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55536799}}, as the Kokuzō during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' states that Inagimi no Mikoto, the brother of {{ill|Katakatane no Mikoto|ja|片堅石命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakatane no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, founder of {{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suruga Kokuzō|qq=|1=Suruga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11573348}}, was appointed Kokuzō of Tōtsuōmi. This record also describes Inagimi as the ancestor of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Agatanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō, and several related clans.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan belonged to the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]]. While the clan's official [[:en:Uji (clan)|Uji]] and {{ill|kabane|ja|姓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kabane|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} remain uncertain, it is believed to have adopted '''Tōtsuōmi''' (遠淡海氏) as its name, with the kabane of '''Nao''' (直), like its related clans {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久努氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/mononobe2g.htm Overview of the Mononobe Clan]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2003. The clan is also associated with the use of "Tachibana" (橘) and the mythical rejuvenating "Ochimizu" (変若水). Several descendants later adopted the {{ill|Tachibana no Ason|ja|橘朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Ason|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} surname.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/monobekz/monobek1.htm Examination of the Mononobe Lineage in the ''Tenson Hongi'']," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2008. == Headquarters == === Main Base === The headquarters of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō were in [[:en:Iwata District, Shizuoka|Iwata District, Tōtōmi]], corresponding to modern [[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata, Shizuoka]]. This area later became the site of the provincial [[:en:Kokufu|kokufu]] and [[:en:Provincial temple|Provincial temple]] of Tōtōmi Province. === Territory === The Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō governed the region known as Tōtsuōmi, later divided into Iwata District and surrounding areas, including {{ill|Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|ja|山香郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|ja|長上郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|qq=|1=Nagakami district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651399}}, and {{ill|Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|ja|長下郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The boundaries of the Kokuzō's territory remain debated, with theories including: * Surroundings of [[:en:Lake Hamana|Lake Hamana]]. * West of the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryū River]]. * From Lake Hamana to the western edge of {{ill|Iwata Plain|ja|磐田原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwata Plain|qq=|1=Iwatahara plateau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106982104}}. * From Lake Hamana to the [[:en:Ōta River|Ōtagawa River]] basin. == Clan Deity == === Shrine Associations === * '''{{ill|Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|ja|淡海國玉神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|qq=}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mitsuke, Iwata|ja|磐田市見付|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke, Iwata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this shrine is the {{ill|sōja|ja|惣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sōja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 総社|1=sōja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1107129}} of Tōtōmi Province and enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. * '''[[:en:Okuni Shrine|Oguni Shrine]]''' : Located in {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|ja|森町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori, Shizuoka|qq=|1=Morimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q854613}}, this shrine is the [[:en:Ichinomiya|Ichinomiya]] of Tōtōmi Province, enshrining {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. === {{ill|Kuji Kokuzō|ja|久自国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuji Kokuzō|qq=|1=Kuji Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369664}}
{{ill|Ōbe clan|ja|大部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbe clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Zō) }} '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō''' (遠淡海国造) refers to the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kokuzō]] of Tōtsuōmi Province, which encompassed the western region of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]. The title is also rendered as Tōtōmi Kokuzō. == Overview == * The [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] mentions the title as '''Tōtōmi Kokuzō'''. The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] refers to it as '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō'''. The progenitor of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō is recorded as {{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|Takehi-Nateru=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Takeruhiratori no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}}, a descendant of [[Ame no Hohi]], linking the lineage to the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|Izumo Province 造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造|lt=Musashi Kokuzō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 无邪志国造}}, and other related clans. * The ''Kokuzō Hongi'' of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' names {{ill|Inagimi no Mikoto|ja|印岐美命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagimi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikaga Shikoo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o命|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55536799}}, as the Kokuzō during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' states that Inagimi no Mikoto, the brother of {{ill|Katakatane no Mikoto|ja|片堅石命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakatane no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, founder of {{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suruga Kokuzō|qq=|1=Suruga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11573348}}, was appointed Kokuzō of Tōtsuōmi. This record also describes Inagimi as the ancestor of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Agatanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō, and several related clans.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan belonged to the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]]. While the clan's official [[:en:Uji (clan)|Uji]] and {{ill|kabane|ja|姓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kabane|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} remain uncertain, it is believed to have adopted '''Tōtsuōmi''' (遠淡海氏) as its name, with the kabane of '''Nao''' (直), like its related clans {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久努氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/mononobe2g.htm Overview of the Mononobe Clan]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2003. The clan is also associated with the use of "Tachibana" (橘) and the mythical rejuvenating "Ochimizu" (変若水). Several descendants later adopted the {{ill|Tachibana no Ason|ja|橘朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Ason|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} surname.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/monobekz/monobek1.htm Examination of the Mononobe Lineage in the ''Tenson Hongi'']," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2008. == Headquarters == === Main Base === The headquarters of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō were in [[:en:Iwata District, Shizuoka|Iwata District, Tōtōmi]], corresponding to modern [[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata, Shizuoka]]. This area later became the site of the provincial [[:en:Kokufu|kokufu]] and [[:en:Provincial temple|Provincial temple]] of Tōtōmi Province. === Territory === The Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō governed the region known as Tōtsuōmi, later divided into Iwata District and surrounding areas, including {{ill|Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|ja|山香郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|ja|長上郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|qq=|1=Nagakami district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651399}}, and {{ill|Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|ja|長下郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The boundaries of the Kokuzō's territory remain debated, with theories including: * Surroundings of [[:en:Lake Hamana|Lake Hamana]]. * West of the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryū River]]. * From Lake Hamana to the western edge of {{ill|Iwata Plain|ja|磐田原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwata Plain|qq=|1=Iwatahara plateau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106982104}}. * From Lake Hamana to the [[:en:Ōta River|Ōtagawa River]] basin. == Clan Deity == === Shrine Associations === * '''{{ill|Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|ja|淡海國玉神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|qq=}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mitsuke, Iwata|ja|磐田市見付|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke, Iwata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this shrine is the {{ill|sōja|ja|惣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sōja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 総社|1=sōja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1107129}} of Tōtōmi Province and enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. * '''[[:en:Okuni Shrine|Oguni Shrine]]''' : Located in {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|ja|森町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori, Shizuoka|qq=|1=Morimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q854613}}, this shrine is the [[:en:Ichinomiya|Ichinomiya]] of Tōtōmi Province, enshrining {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. === Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kochōshizuka Kofun|ja|小銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 銚子塚古墳 (磐田市)}}''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten,'' {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}. * Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan. * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese {{ill|sōja|ja|惣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sōja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 総社|1=sōja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1107129}} of Tōtōmi Province and enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. * '''[[:en:Okuni Shrine|Oguni Shrine]]''' : Located in {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|ja|森町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori, Shizuoka|qq=|1=Morimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q854613}}, this shrine is the [[:en:Ichinomiya|Ichinomiya]] of Tōtōmi Province, enshrining {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. === Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kochōshizuka Kofun|ja|小銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 銚子塚古墳 (磐田市)}}''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten,'' {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=|1=Yoshikawa History,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Publishing|qq=|1=Yamakawa Shuppansha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11468274}}. * "Rekishi Dokuhon" Henshūbu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501312}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kokuzō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造|1=kuni no miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2483673}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub-Shizuoka}} {{translated page|ja|遠淡海国造|version=104194612|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Izumo clan]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Tōtomi Province no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:遠淡海国造]] [[sn:ōtomi Province no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Tōtomi Province no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] {{wikidata link|Q11642135}}

{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suruga Kokuzō|qq=|1=Suruga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11573348}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasa clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺部氏|1=Kanasa clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646498}}
{{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|久努国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno Kokuzō|qq=|1=Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85875681}}
[[:en:Kuno clan|Kuno clan]] (Nao)
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nao)
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koichi Kokuzō|qq=|1=ochinokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652684}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi clan|qq=}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazahaya Kokuzō|qq=|1=kazahayanokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652739}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazahaya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kuji Kokuzō|ja|久自国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuji Kokuzō|qq=|1=Kuji Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369664}}
{{ill|Ōbe clan|ja|大部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbe clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Zō) }} '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō''' (遠淡海国造) refers to the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kokuzō]] of Tōtsuōmi Province, which encompassed the western region of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]. The title is also rendered as Tōtōmi Kokuzō. == Overview == * The [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] mentions the title as '''Tōtōmi Kokuzō'''. The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] refers to it as '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō'''. The progenitor of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō is recorded as {{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|Takehi-Nateru=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Takeruhiratori no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}}, a descendant of [[Ame no Hohi]], linking the lineage to the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|Izumo Province 造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造|lt=Musashi Kokuzō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 无邪志国造}}, and other related clans. * The ''Kokuzō Hongi'' of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' names {{ill|Inagimi no Mikoto|ja|印岐美命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagimi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikaga Shikoo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o命|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55536799}}, as the Kokuzō during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' states that Inagimi no Mikoto, the brother of {{ill|Katakatane no Mikoto|ja|片堅石命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakatane no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, founder of {{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suruga Kokuzō|qq=|1=Suruga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11573348}}, was appointed Kokuzō of Tōtsuōmi. This record also describes Inagimi as the ancestor of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Agatanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō, and several related clans.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan belonged to the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]]. While the clan's official [[:en:Uji (clan)|Uji]] and {{ill|kabane|ja|姓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kabane|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} remain uncertain, it is believed to have adopted '''Tōtsuōmi''' (遠淡海氏) as its name, with the kabane of '''Nao''' (直), like its related clans {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久努氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/mononobe2g.htm Overview of the Mononobe Clan]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2003. The clan is also associated with the use of "Tachibana" (橘) and the mythical rejuvenating "Ochimizu" (変若水). Several descendants later adopted the {{ill|Tachibana no Ason|ja|橘朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Ason|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} surname.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/monobekz/monobek1.htm Examination of the Mononobe Lineage in the ''Tenson Hongi'']," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2008. == Headquarters == === Main Base === The headquarters of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō were in [[:en:Iwata District, Shizuoka|Iwata District, Tōtōmi]], corresponding to modern [[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata, Shizuoka]]. This area later became the site of the provincial [[:en:Kokufu|kokufu]] and [[:en:Provincial temple|Provincial temple]] of Tōtōmi Province. === Territory === The Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō governed the region known as Tōtsuōmi, later divided into Iwata District and surrounding areas, including {{ill|Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|ja|山香郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|ja|長上郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|qq=|1=Nagakami district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651399}}, and {{ill|Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|ja|長下郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The boundaries of the Kokuzō's territory remain debated, with theories including: * Surroundings of [[:en:Lake Hamana|Lake Hamana]]. * West of the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryū River]]. * From Lake Hamana to the western edge of {{ill|Iwata Plain|ja|磐田原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwata Plain|qq=|1=Iwatahara plateau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106982104}}. * From Lake Hamana to the [[:en:Ōta River|Ōtagawa River]] basin. == Clan Deity == === Shrine Associations === * '''{{ill|Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|ja|淡海國玉神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|qq=}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mitsuke, Iwata|ja|磐田市見付|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke, Iwata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this shrine is the {{ill|sōja|ja|惣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sōja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 総社|1=sōja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1107129}} of Tōtōmi Province and enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. * '''[[:en:Okuni Shrine|Oguni Shrine]]''' : Located in {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|ja|森町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori, Shizuoka|qq=|1=Morimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q854613}}, this shrine is the [[:en:Ichinomiya|Ichinomiya]] of Tōtōmi Province, enshrining {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. === Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kochōshizuka Kofun|ja|小銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 銚子塚古墳 (磐田市)}}''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten,'' {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}. * Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan. * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Publishing|qq=|1=Yamakawa Shuppansha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11468274}}. * ''Daijiten of Shintō,'' {{ill|Rinsen Bookstore|ja|臨川書店|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinsen Bookstore|qq=|1=Rinsen Shoten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612454}}. * ''General Overview of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|qq=|1=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha. "Rekishi Dokuhon" Henshūbu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501312}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kokuzō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造|1=kuni no miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2483673}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub-Shizuoka}} {{translated page|ja|遠淡海国造|version=104194612|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Izumo clan]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Tōtomi Province no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:遠淡海国造]] [[sn:ōtomi Province no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q11642135}}

The progenitor of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō is recorded as {{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|Takehi-Nateru=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Takeruhiratori no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}}, a descendant of [[Ame no Hohi]], linking the lineage to the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|Izumo Province 造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造|lt=Musashi Kokuzō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 无邪志国造}}, and other related clans. * The ''Kokuzō Hongi'' of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' names {{ill|Inagimi no Mikoto|ja|印岐美命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagimi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikaga Shikoo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o命|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55536799}}, as the Kokuzō during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' states that Inagimi no Mikoto, the brother of {{ill|Katakatane no Mikoto|ja|片堅石命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakatane no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, founder of {{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suruga Kokuzō|qq=|1=Suruga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11573348}}, was appointed Kokuzō of Tōtsuōmi. This record also describes Inagimi as the ancestor of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Agatanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō, and several related clans.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan belonged to the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]]. While the clan's official [[:en:Uji (clan)|Uji]] and {{ill|kabane|ja|姓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kabane|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} remain uncertain, it is believed to have adopted '''Tōtsuōmi''' (遠淡海氏) as its name, with the kabane of '''Nao''' (直), like its related clans {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久努氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/mononobe2g.htm Overview of the Mononobe Clan]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2003. The clan is also associated with the use of "Tachibana" (橘) and the mythical rejuvenating "Ochimizu" (変若水). Several descendants later adopted the {{ill|Tachibana no Ason|ja|橘朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Ason|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} surname.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/monobekz/monobek1.htm Examination of the Mononobe Lineage in the ''Tenson Hongi'']," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2008. == Headquarters == === Main Base === The headquarters of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō were in [[:en:Iwata District, Shizuoka|Iwata District, Tōtōmi]], corresponding to modern [[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata, Shizuoka]]. This area later became the site of the provincial [[:en:Kokufu|kokufu]] and [[:en:Provincial temple|Provincial temple]] of Tōtōmi Province. === Territory === The Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō governed the region known as Tōtsuōmi, later divided into Iwata District and surrounding areas, including {{ill|Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|ja|山香郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|ja|長上郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|qq=|1=Nagakami district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651399}}, and {{ill|Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|ja|長下郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The boundaries of the Kokuzō's territory remain debated, with theories including: * Surroundings of [[:en:Lake Hamana|Lake Hamana]]. * West of the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryū River]]. * From Lake Hamana to the western edge of {{ill|Iwata Plain|ja|磐田原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwata Plain|qq=|1=Iwatahara plateau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106982104}}. * From Lake Hamana to the [[:en:Ōta River|Ōtagawa River]] basin. == Clan Deity == === Shrine Associations === * '''{{ill|Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|ja|淡海國玉神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|qq=}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mitsuke, Iwata|ja|磐田市見付|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke, Iwata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this shrine is the {{ill|sōja|ja|惣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sōja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 総社|1=sōja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1107129}} of Tōtōmi Province and enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. * '''[[:en:Okuni Shrine|Oguni Shrine]]''' : Located in {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|ja|森町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori, Shizuoka|qq=|1=Morimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q854613}}, this shrine is the [[:en:Ichinomiya|Ichinomiya]] of Tōtōmi Province, enshrining {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. === {{ill|Ōbe clan|ja|大部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbe clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Zō) }} '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō''' (遠淡海国造) refers to the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kokuzō]] of Tōtsuōmi Province, which encompassed the western region of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]. The title is also rendered as Tōtōmi Kokuzō. == Overview == * The [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] mentions the title as '''Tōtōmi Kokuzō'''. While the clan's official [[:en:Uji (clan)|Uji]] and {{ill|kabane|ja|姓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kabane|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} remain uncertain, it is believed to have adopted '''Tōtsuōmi''' (遠淡海氏) as its name, with the kabane of '''Nao''' (直), like its related clans {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久努氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/mononobe2g.htm Overview of the Mononobe Clan]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2003. The clan is also associated with the use of "Tachibana" (橘) and the mythical rejuvenating "Ochimizu" (変若水). Several descendants later adopted the {{ill|Tachibana no Ason|ja|橘朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Ason|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} surname.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/monobekz/monobek1.htm Examination of the Mononobe Lineage in the ''Tenson Hongi'']," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2008. == Headquarters == === Main Base === The headquarters of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō were in [[:en:Iwata District, Shizuoka|Iwata District, Tōtōmi]], corresponding to modern [[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata, Shizuoka]]. {{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|久努国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno Kokuzō|qq=|1=Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85875681}}
[[:en:Kuno clan|Kuno clan]] (Nao)
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nao)
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koichi Kokuzō|qq=|1=ochinokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652684}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi clan|qq=}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazahaya Kokuzō|qq=|1=kazahayanokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652739}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazahaya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kuji Kokuzō|ja|久自国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuji Kokuzō|qq=|1=Kuji Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369664}}
{{ill|Ōbe clan|ja|大部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbe clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Zō) }} '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō''' (遠淡海国造) refers to the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kokuzō]] of Tōtsuōmi Province, which encompassed the western region of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]. The title is also rendered as Tōtōmi Kokuzō. == Overview == * The [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] mentions the title as '''Tōtōmi Kokuzō'''. The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] refers to it as '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō'''. The progenitor of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō is recorded as {{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|Takehi-Nateru=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Takeruhiratori no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}}, a descendant of [[Ame no Hohi]], linking the lineage to the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|Izumo Province 造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造|lt=Musashi Kokuzō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 无邪志国造}}, and other related clans. * The ''Kokuzō Hongi'' of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' names {{ill|Inagimi no Mikoto|ja|印岐美命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagimi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikaga Shikoo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o命|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55536799}}, as the Kokuzō during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' states that Inagimi no Mikoto, the brother of {{ill|Katakatane no Mikoto|ja|片堅石命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakatane no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, founder of {{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suruga Kokuzō|qq=|1=Suruga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11573348}}, was appointed Kokuzō of Tōtsuōmi. This record also describes Inagimi as the ancestor of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Agatanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō, and several related clans.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan belonged to the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]]. While the clan's official [[:en:Uji (clan)|Uji]] and {{ill|kabane|ja|姓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kabane|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} remain uncertain, it is believed to have adopted '''Tōtsuōmi''' (遠淡海氏) as its name, with the kabane of '''Nao''' (直), like its related clans {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久努氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/mononobe2g.htm Overview of the Mononobe Clan]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2003. The clan is also associated with the use of "Tachibana" (橘) and the mythical rejuvenating "Ochimizu" (変若水). Several descendants later adopted the {{ill|Tachibana no Ason|ja|橘朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Ason|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} surname.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/monobekz/monobek1.htm Examination of the Mononobe Lineage in the ''Tenson Hongi'']," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2008. == Headquarters == === Main Base === The headquarters of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō were in [[:en:Iwata District, Shizuoka|Iwata District, Tōtōmi]], corresponding to modern [[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata, Shizuoka]]. This area later became the site of the provincial [[:en:Kokufu|kokufu]] and [[:en:Provincial temple|Provincial temple]] of Tōtōmi Province. === Territory === The Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō governed the region known as Tōtsuōmi, later divided into Iwata District and surrounding areas, including {{ill|Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|ja|山香郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|ja|長上郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|qq=|1=Nagakami district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651399}}, and {{ill|Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|ja|長下郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The boundaries of the Kokuzō's territory remain debated, with theories including: * Surroundings of [[:en:Lake Hamana|Lake Hamana]]. * West of the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryū River]]. * From Lake Hamana to the western edge of {{ill|Iwata Plain|ja|磐田原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwata Plain|qq=|1=Iwatahara plateau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106982104}}. * From Lake Hamana to the [[:en:Ōta River|Ōtagawa River]] basin. == Clan Deity == === Shrine Associations === * '''{{ill|Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|ja|淡海國玉神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|qq=}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mitsuke, Iwata|ja|磐田市見付|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke, Iwata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this shrine is the {{ill|sōja|ja|惣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sōja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 総社|1=sōja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1107129}} of Tōtōmi Province and enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. * '''[[:en:Okuni Shrine|Oguni Shrine]]''' : Located in {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|ja|森町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori, Shizuoka|qq=|1=Morimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q854613}}, this shrine is the [[:en:Ichinomiya|Ichinomiya]] of Tōtōmi Province, enshrining {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. === Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kochōshizuka Kofun|ja|小銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 銚子塚古墳 (磐田市)}}''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten,'' {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}. * Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan. * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Publishing|qq=|1=Yamakawa Shuppansha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11468274}}. * ''Daijiten of Shintō,'' {{ill|Rinsen Bookstore|ja|臨川書店|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinsen Bookstore|qq=|1=Rinsen Shoten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612454}}. * ''General Overview of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|qq=|1=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha. "Rekishi Dokuhon" Henshūbu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501312}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kokuzō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造|1=kuni no miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2483673}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub-Shizuoka}} {{translated page|ja|遠淡海国造|version=104194612|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Izumo clan]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] }} [[ja:遠淡海国造]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q11642135}}

{{ill|Kuji Kokuzō|ja|久自国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuji Kokuzō|qq=|1=Kuji Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369664}}
{{ill|Ōbe clan|ja|大部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbe clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Zō) }} '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō''' (遠淡海国造) refers to the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kokuzō]] of Tōtsuōmi Province, which encompassed the western region of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]. The title is also rendered as Tōtōmi Kokuzō. == Overview == * The [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] mentions the title as '''Tōtōmi Kokuzō'''. The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] refers to it as '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō'''. The progenitor of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō is recorded as {{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|Takehi-Nateru=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Takeruhiratori no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}}, a descendant of [[Ame no Hohi]], linking the lineage to the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|Izumo Province 造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造|lt=Musashi Kokuzō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 无邪志国造}}, and other related clans. * The ''Kokuzō Hongi'' of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' names {{ill|Inagimi no Mikoto|ja|印岐美命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagimi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikaga Shikoo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o命|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55536799}}, as the Kokuzō during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' states that Inagimi no Mikoto, the brother of {{ill|Katakatane no Mikoto|ja|片堅石命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakatane no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, founder of {{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suruga Kokuzō|qq=|1=Suruga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11573348}}, was appointed Kokuzō of Tōtsuōmi. This record also describes Inagimi as the ancestor of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Agatanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō, and several related clans.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan belonged to the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]]. While the clan's official [[:en:Uji (clan)|Uji]] and {{ill|kabane|ja|姓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kabane|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} remain uncertain, it is believed to have adopted '''Tōtsuōmi''' (遠淡海氏) as its name, with the kabane of '''Nao''' (直), like its related clans {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久努氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/mononobe2g.htm Overview of the Mononobe Clan]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2003. The clan is also associated with the use of "Tachibana" (橘) and the mythical rejuvenating "Ochimizu" (変若水). Several descendants later adopted the {{ill|Tachibana no Ason|ja|橘朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Ason|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} surname.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/monobekz/monobek1.htm Examination of the Mononobe Lineage in the ''Tenson Hongi'']," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2008. == Headquarters == === Main Base === The headquarters of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō were in [[:en:Iwata District, Shizuoka|Iwata District, Tōtōmi]], corresponding to modern [[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata, Shizuoka]]. This area later became the site of the provincial [[:en:Kokufu|kokufu]] and [[:en:Provincial temple|Provincial temple]] of Tōtōmi Province. === Territory === The Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō governed the region known as Tōtsuōmi, later divided into Iwata District and surrounding areas, including {{ill|Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|ja|山香郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|ja|長上郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|qq=|1=Nagakami district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651399}}, and {{ill|Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|ja|長下郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The boundaries of the Kokuzō's territory remain debated, with theories including: * Surroundings of [[:en:Lake Hamana|Lake Hamana]]. * West of the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryū River]]. * From Lake Hamana to the western edge of {{ill|Iwata Plain|ja|磐田原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwata Plain|qq=|1=Iwatahara plateau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106982104}}. * From Lake Hamana to the [[:en:Ōta River|Ōtagawa River]] basin. == Clan Deity == === Shrine Associations === * '''{{ill|Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|ja|淡海國玉神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|qq=}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mitsuke, Iwata|ja|磐田市見付|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke, Iwata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this shrine is the {{ill|sōja|ja|惣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sōja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 総社|1=sōja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1107129}} of Tōtōmi Province and enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. * '''[[:en:Okuni Shrine|Oguni Shrine]]''' : Located in {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|ja|森町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori, Shizuoka|qq=|1=Morimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q854613}}, this shrine is the [[:en:Ichinomiya|Ichinomiya]] of Tōtōmi Province, enshrining {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. === Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kochōshizuka Kofun|ja|小銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 銚子塚古墳 {{ill|Kuji Kokuzō|ja|久自国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuji Kokuzō|1=Kuji Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369664}}
{{ill|Ōbe clan|ja|大部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbe clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Zō) }} '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō''' (遠淡海国造) refers to the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kokuzō]] of Tōtsuōmi Province, which encompassed the western region of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]. The title is also rendered as Tōtōmi Kokuzō. == Overview == * The [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] mentions the title as '''Tōtōmi Kokuzō'''. The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] refers to it as '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō'''. The progenitor of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō is recorded as {{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|Takehi-Nateru=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Takeruhiratori no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}}, a descendant of [[Ame no Hohi]], linking the lineage to the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|Izumo Province 造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造|lt=Musashi Kokuzō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 无邪志国造}}, and other related clans. * The ''Kokuzō Hongi'' of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' names {{ill|Inagimi no Mikoto|ja|印岐美命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagimi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikaga Shikoo|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o命|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55536799}}, as the Kokuzō during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' states that Inagimi no Mikoto, the brother of {{ill|Katakatane no Mikoto|ja|片堅石命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakatane no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, founder of {{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suruga Kokuzō|1=Suruga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11573348}}, was appointed Kokuzō of Tōtsuōmi. This record also describes Inagimi as the ancestor of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Agatanushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō, and several related clans.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan belonged to the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]]. While the clan's official [[:en:Uji (clan)|Uji]] and {{ill|kabane|ja|姓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kabane|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} remain uncertain, it is believed to have adopted '''Tōtsuōmi''' (遠淡海氏) as its name, with the kabane of '''Nao''' (直), like its related clans {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久努氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saya clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/mononobe2g.htm Overview of the Mononobe Clan]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2003. The clan is also associated with the use of "Tachibana" (橘) and the mythical rejuvenating "Ochimizu" (変若水). Several descendants later adopted the {{ill|Tachibana no Ason|ja|橘朝臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} surname.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/monobekz/monobek1.htm Examination of the Mononobe Lineage in the ''Tenson Hongi'']," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2008. == Headquarters == === Main Base === The headquarters of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō were in [[:en:Iwata District, Shizuoka|Iwata District, Tōtōmi]], corresponding to modern [[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata, Shizuoka]]. This area later became the site of the provincial [[:en:Kokufu|kokufu]] and [[:en:Provincial temple|Provincial temple]] of Tōtōmi Province. === Territory === The Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō governed the region known as Tōtsuōmi, later divided into Iwata District and surrounding areas, including {{ill|Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|ja|山香郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|ja|長上郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|1=Nagakami district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651399}}, and {{ill|Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|ja|長下郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The boundaries of the Kokuzō's territory remain debated, with theories including: * Surroundings of [[:en:Lake Hamana|Lake Hamana]]. * West of the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryū River]]. * From Lake Hamana to the western edge of {{ill|Iwata Plain|ja|磐田原|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwata Plain|1=Iwatahara plateau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106982104}}. * From Lake Hamana to the [[:en:Ōta River|Ōtagawa River]] basin. == Clan Deity == === Shrine Associations === * '''{{ill|Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|ja|淡海國玉神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|qq=}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mitsuke, Iwata|ja|磐田市見付|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke, Iwata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this shrine is the {{ill|sōja|ja|惣社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sōja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 総社|1=sōja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1107129}} of Tōtōmi Province and enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. * '''[[:en:Okuni Shrine|Oguni Shrine]]''' : Located in {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|ja|森町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori, Shizuoka|1=Morimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q854613}}, this shrine is the [[:en:Ichinomiya|Ichinomiya]] of Tōtōmi Province, enshrining {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. === Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kochōshizuka Kofun|ja|小銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 銚子塚古墳 (磐田市)}}''' == References == This area later became the site of the provincial [[:en:Kokufu|kokufu]] and [[:en:Provincial temple|Provincial temple]] of Tōtōmi Province. === Territory === The Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō governed the region known as Tōtsuōmi, later divided into Iwata District and surrounding areas, including {{ill|Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|ja|山香郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|ja|長上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|1=Nagakami district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651399}}, and {{ill|Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|ja|長下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The boundaries of the Kokuzō's territory remain debated, with theories including: * Surroundings of [[:en:Lake Hamana|Lake Hamana]]. * West of the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryū River]]. * From Lake Hamana to the western edge of {{ill|Iwata Plain|ja|磐田原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwata Plain|1=Iwatahara plateau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106982104}}. * From Lake Hamana to the [[:en:Ōta River|Ōtagawa River]] basin. == Clan Deity == === Shrine Associations === * '''{{ill|Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|ja|淡海國玉神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|qq=}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mitsuke, Iwata|ja|磐田市見付|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke, Iwata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this shrine is the {{ill|sōja|ja|惣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sōja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 総社|1=sōja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1107129}} of Tōtōmi Province and enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. * '''[[:en:Okuni Shrine|Oguni Shrine]]''' : Located in {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|ja|森町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori, Shizuoka|1=Morimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q854613}}, this shrine is the [[:en:Ichinomiya|Ichinomiya]] of Tōtōmi Province, enshrining {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. === Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kochōshizuka Kofun|ja|小銚子塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 銚子塚古墳 (磐田市)}}''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten,'' {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}. * Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan. * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa [[ja:遠淡海国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11642135]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese clan |Name = Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō |Crest = |Crest Name = |Main Surname = [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] |Founder = {{ill|Inagimi no Mikoto|ja|印岐美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagimi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Tenson|ja|天孫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson|1=Posterity of Heaven|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108278353}}) |Origin = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] |Base = Tōtsuōmi Province (later [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]) |Notable Figures = See {{section link|#Notable Figures}} |Branches = (Parentheses denote clan suffix)
{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suruga Kokuzō|1=Suruga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11573348}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasa clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺部氏|1=Kanasa clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646498}}
{{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|久努国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno Kokuzō|1=Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85875681}}
[[:en:Kuno clan|Kuno clan]] (Nao)
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saya clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nao)
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koichi Kokuzō|1=ochinokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652684}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi clan|qq=}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazahaya Kokuzō|1=kazahayanokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652739}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazahaya clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kuji Kokuzō|ja|久自国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuji Kokuzō|1=Kuji Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369664}}
{{ill|Ōbe clan|ja|大部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbe clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Zō) }} '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō''' (遠淡海国造) refers to the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kokuzō]] of Tōtsuōmi Province, which encompassed the western region of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]. The title is also rendered as Tōtōmi Kokuzō. == Overview == * The [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] mentions the title as '''Tōtōmi Kokuzō'''. The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] refers to it as '''Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō'''. The progenitor of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō is recorded as {{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|Takehi-Nateru=Takehi-Nateru|lt=Takeruhiratori no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}}, a descendant of [[Ame no Hohi]], linking the lineage to the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|Izumo Province 造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造|lt=Musashi Kokuzō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 无邪志国造}}, and other related clans. * The ''Kokuzō Hongi'' of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' names {{ill|Inagimi no Mikoto|ja|印岐美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagimi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikaga Shikoo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o命|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55536799}}, as the Kokuzō during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' states that Inagimi no Mikoto, the brother of {{ill|Katakatane no Mikoto|ja|片堅石命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakatane no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, founder of {{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suruga Kokuzō|1=Suruga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11573348}}, was appointed Kokuzō of Tōtsuōmi. This record also describes Inagimi as the ancestor of {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Agatanushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō, and several related clans.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan belonged to the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]]. While the clan's official [[:en:Uji (clan)|Uji]] and {{ill|kabane|ja|姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kabane|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} remain uncertain, it is believed to have adopted '''Tōtsuōmi''' (遠淡海氏) as its name, with the kabane of '''Nao''' (直), like its related clans {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久努氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saya clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/sizokugairan/mononobe2g.htm Overview of the Mononobe Clan]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2003. The clan is also associated with the use of "Tachibana" (橘) and the mythical rejuvenating "Ochimizu" (変若水). Several descendants later adopted the {{ill|Tachibana no Ason|ja|橘朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} surname.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/monobekz/monobek1.htm Examination of the Mononobe Lineage in the ''Tenson Hongi'']," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2008. == Headquarters == === Main Base === The headquarters of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō were in [[:en:Iwata District, Shizuoka|Iwata District, Tōtōmi]], corresponding to modern [[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata, Shizuoka]]. This area later became the site of the provincial [[:en:Kokufu|kokufu]] and [[:en:Provincial temple|Provincial temple]] of Tōtōmi Province. === Territory === The Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō governed the region known as Tōtsuōmi, later divided into Iwata District and surrounding areas, including {{ill|Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|ja|山香郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|ja|長上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|1=Nagakami district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651399}}, and {{ill|Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|ja|長下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The boundaries of the Kokuzō's territory remain debated, with theories including: * Surroundings of [[:en:Lake Hamana|Lake Hamana]]. * West of the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryū River]]. * From Lake Hamana to the western edge of {{ill|Iwata Plain|ja|磐田原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwata Plain|1=Iwatahara plateau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106982104}}. * From Lake Hamana to the [[:en:Ōta River|Ōtagawa River]] basin. == Clan Deity == === Shrine Associations === * '''{{ill|Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|ja|淡海國玉神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|qq=}}''' : Located in {{ill|Mitsuke, Iwata|ja|磐田市見付|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuke, Iwata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this shrine is the {{ill|sōja|ja|惣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sōja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 総社|1=sōja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1107129}} of Tōtōmi Province and enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. * '''[[:en:Okuni Shrine|Oguni Shrine]]''' : Located in {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|ja|森町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori, Shizuoka|1=Morimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q854613}}, this shrine is the [[:en:Ichinomiya|Ichinomiya]] of Tōtōmi Province, enshrining {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. === Burial Sites === * '''{{ill|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kochōshizuka Kofun|ja|小銚子塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 銚子塚古墳 (磐田市)}}''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten,'' {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}. * Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan. * ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Publishing|1=Yamakawa Shuppansha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11468274}}. * ''Daijiten of Shintō,'' {{ill|Rinsen Bookstore|ja|臨川書店|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinsen Bookstore|1=Rinsen Shoten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612454}}. * ''General Overview of Japanese History,'' {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|1=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha. "Rekishi Dokuhon" Henshūbu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501312}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kokuzō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造|1=kuni no miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2483673}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub-Shizuoka}} {{translated page|ja|遠淡海国造|version=104194612|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Izumo clan]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] }} [[ja:遠淡海国造]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] {{wikidata link|Q11642135}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

The clan is also associated with the use of "Tachibana" (橘) and the mythical rejuvenating "Ochimizu" (変若水). Several descendants later adopted the {{ill|Tachibana no Ason|ja|橘朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} surname.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/monobekz/monobek1.htm Examination of the Mononobe Lineage in the ''Tenson Hongi'']," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2008. == Headquarters == === Main Base === The headquarters of the Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō were in [[:en:Iwata District, Shizuoka|Iwata District, Tōtōmi]], corresponding to modern [[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata, Shizuoka]]. This area later became the site of the provincial [[:en:Kokufu|kokufu]] and [[:en:Provincial temple|Provincial temple]] of Tōtōmi Province. === Territory === The Tōtsuōmi Kokuzō governed the region known as Tōtsuōmi, later divided into Iwata District and surrounding areas, including {{ill|Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|ja|山香郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakō District, Tōtōmi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|ja|長上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagakami District, Tōtōmi|1=Nagakami district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651399}}, and {{ill|Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|ja|長下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagashimo District, Tōtōmi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The boundaries of the Kokuzō's territory remain debated, with theories including: * Surroundings of [[:en:Lake Hamana|Lake Hamana]]. * West of the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryū River]]. * From Lake Hamana to the western edge of {{ill|Iwata Plain|ja|磐田原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwata Plain|1=Iwatahara plateau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106982104}}. * From Lake Hamana to the [[:en:Ōta River|Ōtagawa River]] basin. == Clan Deity == === Shrine Associations === * '''{{ill|Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|ja|淡海國玉神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi Kunitama Shrine|qq=}}''' : Located in redirects to 総社|1=sōja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1107129}} of Tōtōmi Province and enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. * '''[[:en:Okuni Shrine|Oguni Shrine]]''' : Located in {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|ja|森町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori, Shizuoka|1=Morimachi|comment=changed title based on
